# CLAUDE.md тАФ Japanese SRS Study App

This file is for AI assistants (Claude and others) working on this codebase. It explains the architecture, data contracts, and the key rules that keep the app and user progress in sync across builds.

## What this project is

A single-file HTML spaced repetition app for Atsuko-san's A0/A1 Japanese class at Upstate International, Greenville SC. The owner is Parker (spsingleton.gwb@gmail.com). It may also be used by classmates.

**The app is never hand-edited.** `index.html` is generated by `build_app.py` from JSON source files. Always edit the source files or `build_app.py`, then rebuild.

## How to rebuild

```bash
cd /path/to/japanese/study-app
python3 build_app.py
```

After the build, verify with: `node --check` on the extracted `<script>` block (see README for the one-liner).

## Architecture

```
build_app.py          Python build script тАФ reads all JSON, writes index.html
index.html            Generated single-file app; do not edit by hand
data/                 JSON source files (one per vocabulary category)
../flashcards/        adjectives_vocab.json shared here (also used for printable cards)
```

### How build_app.py works

1. Loads all JSON source files.
2. Constructs a `decks` list of Python dicts, each with `id`, `name`, `icon`, `type`, and `cards`.
3. Each card dict has: `id`, `front`, `frontSub`, `frontLabel`, `back`, `backSub`, `notes`.
4. `json.dumps()` the decks list into the HTML template as `const DECKS = ...`.
5. Writes the full HTML/CSS/JS to `index.html`.

The entire app state (SRS schedules) lives in `localStorage['jp_srs_v2']` as a plain object keyed by card ID. Favorites live separately in `localStorage['jp_srs_favs_v1']` as a JSON array of card IDs. Export/Import now writes a versioned object `{ _format:'jp_srs_backup_v1', progress, favorites }`; import still accepts the legacy plain progress-map format for backward compatibility.

**Favorites** (`тШЕ`): toggled from the study-screen star or the тШЖ in browse rows. A "Favorites" banner on home opens a virtual `__favs__` deck (handled in `resolveCards`/`cardsForDecks`/`deckStats`/`browseDeckView`) that is browsable and studyable like any deck.

**Report an issue**: a button on the study screen and in each browse row opens a modal, then launches a pre-filled `mailto:` to `REPORT_EMAIL` (top of the REPORT section in the JS) containing the user's note plus deck/front/prompt/answer/notes/card-ID. No backend.

**Build-template gotcha:** the JS lives inside a Python `r"""..."""` template. When editing via tools, a literal `\n` in a JS string can be mis-encoded into a real newline (breaking the string). Prefer `String.fromCharCode(10)` + `Array.join` for multi-line strings, and single-quoted JS strings with double-quoted HTML attributes inside (never `\"`).

## The most important rule: card IDs are immutable

User progress is stored as `{ [cardId]: { interval, ef, reps, due } }`. If a card's `id` changes between builds, the app treats it as a new unseen card and the scheduling history is lost.

**Never rename an existing card `id`. Only add new IDs.**

### ID conventions

Auto-generated (safe to leave alone):
- `adj_<hiragana>` тАФ adjective vocab cards. **Homophones** (same hiragana, different kanji тАФ e.g. уБВуБдуБД = хОЪуБД/цЪСуБД/чЖ▒уБД) are disambiguated with a kanji suffix (`adj_уБВуБдуБД_хОЪуБД`) by `adj_safe_id()`; non-colliding words keep the bare `adj_<hiragana>` form. Without this, the three уБВуБдуБД would share one localStorage progress key.
- `vvoc_<verb_id>` тАФ verb vocabulary cards
- `vconj_<verb_id>_<form>` тАФ verb conjugation (form = masu/masen/te/ta)
- `adjconj_<type>_<hiragana>_<form>` тАФ adjective conjugation

Manually assigned in JSON (must be stable):
- Vocab cards in `data/vocab_*.json` тАФ set in the JSON under `cards[].id`
- Recap cards in `data/weekly_recap_*.json` тАФ set in JSON; use a date prefix like `recap_0611_`

## JSON data schema

### Vocabulary files (`data/vocab_*.json`)

```json
{
  "id": "deck_id",
  "name": "Deck Display Name",
  "icon": "emoji",
  "groups": [
    {
      "name": "Group Name",
      "cards": [
        {
          "id": "unique_stable_id",
          "front": "hiragana or Japanese text shown on front",
          "frontSub": "optional kanji or secondary text (front)",
          "frontLabel": "optional grammar prompt e.g. 'тЖТ polite form?'",
          "back": "English translation or answer",
          "backSub": "optional romaji or secondary answer text",
          "notes": "optional hint shown on back of card"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

### Weekly recap files (`data/weekly_recap_YYYY_MM_DD.json`)

Same structure as vocab files, plus top-level metadata:

```json
{
  "id": "recap_YYYY_MM_DD",
  "name": "Class Recap тАФ Month Day",
  "icon": "ЁЯУЕ",
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "topics_covered": ["уБо particle", "уБЯуБД form", "..."],
  "homework": "Description of homework assignment",
  "next_class": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "summary": [ {"h": "Section heading", "items": ["bullet", "bullet"]} ],
  "groups": [ ... same as vocab ... ]
}
```

The build script auto-discovers all `weekly_recap_*.json` files via glob, sorted by filename (which sorts by date). Each becomes a "Class Review" deck shown at the top of the home screen.

### Adjectives (`../flashcards/adjectives_vocab.json`)

```json
{
  "categories": [
    {
      "type": "i",
      "words": [
        {
          "hiragana": "уБЯуБЛуБД",
          "kanji": "щлШуБД",
          "romaji": "takai",
          "english": "tall; expensive",
          "notes": "optional"
        }
      ]
    },
    { "type": "na", "words": [ ... ] }
  ]
}
```

The `уБДуБД / уВИуБД` adjective (good) is irregular: its casual forms use stem `уВИ` (уВИуБПуБкуБД / уВИуБЛуБгуБЯ / уВИуБПуБкуБЛуБгуБЯ), not `уБДуБП-`. This irregularity also applies to **compounds ending in this уБДуБД** тАФ `уБВуБЯуБ╛уБМуБДуБД тЖТ уБВуБЯуБ╛уБМуВИуБПуБкуБД`, `уБкуБЛуБМуБДуБД тЖТ уБкуБЛуБМуВИуБПуБкуБД`, `уБЛуБгуБУуБДуБД тЖТ уБЛуБгуБУуВИуБПуБкуБД`. The helper `i_adj_info()` in `build_app.py` handles this. Note the exception set `REGULAR_II_ADJECTIVES` (currently `уБЛуВПуБДуБД`): words that end in уБДуБД but are ordinary i-adjectives (`уБЛуВПуБДуБД тЖТ уБЛуВПуБДуБПуБкуБД`, not уБЛуВПуВИуБПуБкуБД). Regular i-adjectives drop exactly one trailing `уБД` (never use `rstrip("уБД")`, which over-strips double-уБД words). Slash entries like `уБЧуВЗуБгуБ▒уБД / уБЧуБКуБЛуВЙуБД` conjugate from the first form.

### Verbs (`data/verbs.json`)

```json
{
  "verbs": [
    {
      "id": "taberu",
      "dict": "уБЯуБ╣уВЛ",
      "kanji": "щгЯуБ╣уВЛ",
      "english": "to eat",
      "type": "ru",
      "notes": "",
      "forms": {
        "masu":  "уБЯуБ╣уБ╛уБЩ",
        "masen": "уБЯуБ╣уБ╛уБЫуВУ",
        "te":    "уБЯуБ╣уБж",
        "ta":    "уБЯуБ╣уБЯ"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

`type` is one of: `ru` (Ru-regular/Group II), `u` (U-regular/Group I), `suru` (уБЩуВЛ compound), `irreg` (irregular). All conjugated forms are **hardcoded** in `forms` тАФ do not try to compute them algorithmically, as exceptions like уБДуБПтЖТуБДуБгуБж are too numerous.

## SRS algorithm

SM-2 variant, same as Anki. Key parameters per card in localStorage:
- `interval` тАФ days until next review
- `ef` тАФ ease factor (starts at 2.5, min 1.3)
- `reps` тАФ consecutive successful reviews
- `due` тАФ Unix timestamp (ms) when next due

Rating mapping: `[Again=0, Hard=1, Good=2, Easy=3]` тЖТ quality scores `[1, 2, 4, 5]`. Quality < 3 resets `reps` and `interval` to 1.

New/unseen cards have `reps=0` and `due=0` (always due). Sessions batch up to 20 new cards at a time when no due cards exist.

## CSS variables

```css
--navy:  #1a1a2e   /* primary dark, card backs, nav */
--red:   #e63946   /* accents, due counts, Again button */
--blue:  #457b9d   /* Easy button, learning stat */
--green: #2a9d8f   /* Good button, mastered stat, progress bar */
--yellow:#e9c46a   /* Hard button */
--gray:  #8d99ae   /* secondary text, labels */
```

## Screens

| Screen ID | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `screen-intro` | Landing page, shown once (flag: `jp_srs_intro_seen` in localStorage) |
| `screen-home` | Deck list with stats and recap banners |
| `screen-study` | Active card session (also used for single-card practice via `practiceCard`) |
| `screen-complete` | Session summary |
| `screen-browse` | Browse all cards in one deck; reveal-on-tap answers, per-card status, single-card practice. Reached via the тШ░ button on a deck/recap or by tapping a search result (which deep-links to the exact card via `openBrowse(deckId, cardId)`). |

Navigation is purely JS (`showScreen(id)`). No routing library.

## Conjugation decks

Per class feedback, verbs and adjectives are kept **separate**. There are four conjugation decks (all `type: 'conjugation'`), shown under two home sections тАФ "Verb Conjugation" and "Adjective Conjugation". Home filtering keys off the id suffix: `_verbs` vs `_adj`.

- **`conj_patterns_verbs` / `conj_patterns_adj` тАФ Patterns & Exceptions.** Hand-authored concept/rule cards from `data/conjugation_concepts.json` (vocab-file format; ids `concept_*`). The single JSON is split into the two decks by group name in `build_app.py` (`VERB_CONCEPT_GROUPS` / `ADJ_CONCEPT_GROUPS`). This is where the *rules* live (уБД-adj negative, the уБДуБДтЖТуВИ irregularity, u-verb уБж-form branches, уБДуБП/уБЩуВЛ/уБПуВЛ). Edit the JSON to add/refine concepts.
- **`conj_drills_verbs` / `conj_drills_adj` тАФ Conjugation Drills.** Built from **curated subsets** (`CURATED_VERB_IDS`, `CURATED_I_ADJ`, `CURATED_NA_ADJ`) so you can't autopilot one pattern. Cards keep their original `vconj_*` / `adjconj_*` ids (progress preserved). The English meaning is on the **back** (`backSub`); the front shows only the base form + prompt. Each drill deck is a fixed-seed shuffle (`random.Random(...)`) to interleave forms while staying byte-reproducible.

To include a word in the drills, add it to the relevant `CURATED_*` list (it must exist in `verbs.json` / `adjectives_vocab.json`). The old combined `conj_drills`/`conj_patterns` and per-form `verb_conj_*`/`adj_conj_*` decks no longer exist.

## Concept summaries

Any deck may carry an optional top-level `summary`: a list of `{"h": heading, "items": [bullets]}` sections. `buildSummaryHtml()` renders it in two places: (1) a collapsible "ЁЯУЦ Concept summary" panel atop that deck's **browse** view (`#browse-summary`), and (2) a **ЁЯУЦ popup reachable mid-study** тАФ a button in the study header (`#study-summary-btn`, shown only when the session's single deck has a summary, via `sessionSummaryDeck()`) opens `#summary-modal`. Use summaries for concept-heavy decks (conjugation, counting, recaps) тАФ not plain vocab. Drill-deck summaries are defined in `build_app.py` (`VERB_DRILL_SUMMARY`, `ADJ_DRILL_SUMMARY`); counting/recap summaries live in their JSON. The build passes `summary` through for vocab and recap decks.

## Counting deck

`data/vocab_counting.json` (deck id `counting`, a normal vocab deck) covers numbers 1тАУ10 and beyond, the native `уАЬуБд` counter, and the counters ф║║уГ╗цн│уГ╗цЬмуГ╗хАЛуГ╗цЮЪуГ╗хМ╣уГ╗щЪОуГ╗хЫЮ with their sound changes (`уБДуБгуБ╜уВУ`, `уБХуВУуБ│уБН`, `уБХуВУуБМуБД`, тАж). Card ids are `count_*`. Note 3щЪО (`уБХуВУуБМуБД`) vs 3хЫЮ (`уБХуВУуБЛуБД`) is an intentional teaching contrast.

## Kana decks (hiragana / katakana)

Two decks (`kana_hiragana`, `kana_katakana`, `type: 'kana'`) are generated entirely in `build_app.py` from the hand-authored `KANA_BASIC` / `KANA_DAKUTEN` / `KANA_YOON` tables тАФ no JSON source files. Each covers the full set: 46 basic goj┼лon + 25 dakuten/handakuten + 33 y┼Нon combos = **104 cards each**. Katakana glyphs are derived from the hiragana table via `to_kata()` (a fixed +0x60 codepoint shift), so only hiragana is authored.

- **Card front** = the kana glyph; **back** = Hepburn romaji. Cards carry `tag: 'Reading'` so the study front shows "Reading" instead of "Vocabulary".
- **IDs** are `hira_<key>` / `kata_<key>` where `<key>` is the romaji, except the four homophones which get explicit distinct keys in the tables: уБШ=`ji`, уБв=`dji`, уБЪ=`zu`, уБе=`dzu`. Never change these keys (progress is keyed on them).
- Each deck has a concept `summary` (`HIRAGANA_SUMMARY` / `KATAKANA_SUMMARY`) shown in browse + mid-study.
- Home renders them in their own "Hiragana & Katakana" section (`deck-list-kana`, filtered by `type==='kana'`), placed above Vocabulary.

To edit the kana sets, change the tables in `build_app.py` and rebuild. Kana cards deliberately get **no** `romaji` field (romaji is already the answer), so the romaji-front toggle never affects them.

## Romaji-on-front toggle (vocabulary only)

`build_app.py` attaches a derived Hepburn `romaji` field to every **vocab-type** card (from its kana `front`, via `kana_to_romaji()`). The home screen has a "Romaji on front" switch in the Vocabulary section header, persisted in `localStorage['jp_srs_romaji_front']` (**default off**). When on, the romaji is shown on the **front** of the flashcard (study screen `#cf-romaji`, and each browse row `.browse-romaji`).

Scoping is automatic: only vocab decks get a `romaji` field, so kana/conjugation/recap cards never show front romaji even inside a mixed `__all__`/`__favs__` session. `kana_to_romaji()` handles y┼Нon combos, the small tsu (уБгтЖТdoubled consonant), the long-vowel mark (уГ╝), and loanword small-vowel combos (уГХуВгтЖТfi). Derived readings are verified to match the curated `backSub` romaji on the noun decks (modulo spacing). The toggle updates the current study card / browse view live via `updateFrontRomaji()` / `renderBrowse()`.

## Common tasks for future sessions

**Add a new week's recap:** Create `data/weekly_recap_YYYY_MM_DD.json`, rebuild.

**Add a new verb:** Append to `verbs.json` with all four hardcoded forms (adds it to the Verbs-vocabulary deck). To also drill it, add its id to `CURATED_VERB_IDS`. Rebuild.

**Add/refine a conjugation rule card:** Edit `data/conjugation_concepts.json`, rebuild.

**Add a new vocabulary category:** Create a new `data/vocab_*.json` and add it to the `for d in [...]` list near the top of `build_app.py`, rebuild.

**Change app layout/style:** Edit the HTML template string in `build_app.py`. Remember: no apostrophes in single-quoted JS strings (use double-quoted strings instead). Rebuild and validate JS syntax before shipping.

**Debug progress issues:** Open browser DevTools тЖТ Application тЖТ Local Storage тЖТ look for key `jp_srs_v2`. The value is a JSON object mapping card IDs to scheduling data.
